name: Avalon Scabbard
description: The Avalon Scabbard, also known as Caliburn's Sheath, is a legendary artifact from Arthurian mythology. It is intrinsically linked to Excalibur, King Arthur's sword, and said to possess powers that surpassed even the blade itself. The scabbard was forged on the mystical Isle of Avalon, a place of magic and healing, by otherworldly beings, often associated with the Lady of the Lake or Morgan le Fay (depending on the version of the legend). Its most significant power was its ability to prevent its wearer from losing blood in battle, rendering them virtually invincible and tireless. This protection extended to the point that Arthur could sustain grievous wounds and still continue fighting with undiminished strength. The loss of the scabbard, often through treachery or misunderstanding, is a pivotal moment in the Arthurian legends, marking the beginning of Arthur's downfall. Some versions claim Morgan le Fay stole it, while others say it was cast back into the lake after the Battle of Camlann. The scabbard represents not only physical protection but also the vulnerability of Arthur's reign and the fragility of peace and order in Camelot. Without it, Arthur was mortal and susceptible to defeat, highlighting the importance of vigilance and loyalty in maintaining power. The scabbard's disappearance symbolizes the loss of magic and enchantment from the world, ushering in an era of decline and ultimately, the end of Arthur's kingdom. The scabbard is more than just a protective sheath; it's a symbol of Arthur's strength, vulnerability, and the ephemeral nature of power.
subtitle: The Sheath that Grants Invincibility
material: Unknown, possibly enchanted metal or hide
origin: Forged on the Isle of Avalon by magical beings
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly at the bottom of a lake or in Avalon

keyMyths.title: The Scabbard's Power
keyMyths.description: The Avalon Scabbard prevented its wearer from losing blood, making them nearly invincible in battle. Losing the scabbard made Arthur vulnerable and led to his downfall.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

keyMyths.title: The Theft of the Scabbard
keyMyths.description: In some versions, Morgan le Fay steals the scabbard, highlighting her treachery and contributing to Arthur's defeat. In other versions, it is lost or discarded after the Battle of Camlann.

sources.author: Thomas Malory
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends.

sources.author: Geoffrey of Monmouth
sources.description: An early and influential source for Arthurian lore.
sources.text: An early and influential source for Arthurian lore.
